candidates.current_note: README/search result describes Playwright as a web testing/automation framework and notes a CLI aimed at coding agents.
candidates.description: Playwright is a framework for Web Testing and Automation. It allows testing Chromium, Firefox and WebKit with a single API.
candidates.recommended_role: deterministic fallback for captures, assertions, screenshots, and replayable browser fixtures
candidates.risk_gate: browser install/start still requires approval; public actions and signed-in state need service-worker gates

candidates.current_note: README/search result describes Browser Use 0.13 as a beta Rust-core agent with browser harness, persistent tools, and recovery loops.
candidates.description: 🌐 Make websites accessible for AI agents. Automate tasks online with ease.
candidates.recommended_role: primary exploratory browser-agent adapter behind browser_read_only_session and public_action gates
candidates.risk_gate: browser_read_only_worker_policy plus signed approval; model/API and cloud keys gated separately

candidates.current_note: README/search result describes natural-language plus code browser control, with act/extract/observe style reliability.
candidates.recommended_role: best TypeScript SDK pattern for structured extract/observe/act behind service-worker packets
candidates.risk_gate: Browserbase/cloud/API keys/payment gates if remote sessions are used; local CDP only after approval

candidates.current_note: README/search result describes an MCP server using structured accessibility snapshots instead of screenshots.
candidates.recommended_role: MCP option for accessibility-tree read-only captures and deterministic browser interactions
candidates.risk_gate: MCP registry gate, prompt-injection/RCE review, no browser_run_code-style tool exposure without denylist and signed scope

candidates.current_note: README/search result describes a fast native Rust browser automation CLI for AI agents, with local install or Browser Use cloud provider mode.
candidates.description: Browser automation CLI for AI agents
candidates.recommended_role: evaluate as compact command surface for browser workers after local install/start approval
candidates.risk_gate: Chrome install/start, cloud API key, and provider selection all require explicit approval

candidates.current_note: README/search result describes an open-source browser API for AI agents/apps that manages sessions, pages, and browser processes.
candidates.description: 🔥 Open Source Browser API for AI Agents & Apps. Steel Browser is a batteries-included browser sandbox that lets you automate the web without worrying about infrastructure.
candidates.recommended_role: study session API, sandbox, and persistent profile model for future worker pools
candidates.risk_gate: cloud/self-host runtime, credential injection, proxies, CAPTCHA/stealth claims, and payment/API keys require stronger CRO approval

candidates.current_note: README/search result describes a thin editable CDP harness connected directly to a real browser.
candidates.description: Browser Harness | Self-healing harness that enables LLMs to complete any task.
candidates.recommended_role: reference for CDP session lifecycle and self-healing harness patterns
candidates.risk_gate: high autonomy; require domain allowlist, no credential entry, egress logging, and kill switch before any browser start

candidates.current_note: README/search result describes LLM plus computer-vision browser workflow automation and a Playwright-compatible SDK.
candidates.description: Automate browser based workflows with AI
candidates.recommended_role: study workflow-builder UX and evidence capture; not a default local runtime
candidates.risk_gate: AGPL, Docker/service footprint, credentials/download/form-fill risk, and account/workflow execution approvals required

candidates.current_note: Python port of Stagehand-style AI browser automation.
candidates.recommended_role: possible Python-lane bridge if TypeScript Stagehand is adopted first
candidates.risk_gate: same as Stagehand plus maturity gap; do not adopt before TypeScript core review

candidates.current_note: README/search result describes HyperAgent as Playwright supercharged with AI and natural-language commands.
candidates.recommended_role: compare natural-language wrapper ergonomics against Stagehand and Browser Use
candidates.risk_gate: cloud/browser infra and API key gates likely required for production use

candidates.current_note: README/search result describes an open-source Chrome extension for AI-powered web automation with multi-agent workflows and BYO model key.
candidates.description: Open-Source Chrome extension for AI-powered web automation. Run multi-agent workflows using your own LLM API key. Alternative to OpenAI Operator.
candidates.recommended_role: study extension UX and multi-agent controls; do not use on personal browser profile
candidates.risk_gate: extension install, user browser data exposure, API keys, and public/account actions require explicit approval and isolated profile

candidates.current_note: README/search result describes Hyperbrowser MCP as tools for scraping, extraction, crawling, and access to browser agents.
candidates.description: A MCP server implementation for hyperbrowser
candidates.recommended_role: watch as cloud-browser MCP integration, not local-first core
candidates.risk_gate: MCP registry, API key, scraping/crawling policy, cloud billing, and browser action approvals required

candidates.current_note: README/search result describes an open-source agentic Chromium fork with local-model/BYO-key positioning.
candidates.description: 🌐 The open-source Agentic browser; alternative to ChatGPT Atlas, Perplexity Comet, Dia.
candidates.recommended_role: track privacy-first browser-native agent ideas; avoid adopting full browser fork into worker stack now
candidates.risk_gate: AGPL, browser fork install/update trust, profile isolation, secrets, and extension/tool surface review required

method: Read-only public GitHub API metadata plus primary repository README/search-result evidence; no clone, install, runtime, browser session, API key, login, account action, public action, payment, wallet, or model/API call.
purpose: Refresh browser/computer-use worker infrastructure candidates for the agent company without installing, starting, or approving any browser worker.

recommendation.foundation: Use Playwright as the deterministic browser automation base after explicit browser install/start approval.
recommendation.next_local_build: Create browser_worker_adapter_contract_v1 that maps any approved browser runtime to domain allowlists, action-class denylist, screenshot/DOM capture artifacts, session lifecycle records, and egress trace events before a browser starts.

title: Browser Worker Stack Radar Wave 18
